sections.content:
I see the key difference now. At step 16 (successful SPACE):
- N is split into 4 pieces: rows 29-31 (27), rows 35-37 cols 41-43 (9), rows 35-37 cols 47-49 (9), rows 47-49 cols 44-46 (9)
- W is at rows 32-46, cols 38-52
- The N pieces are positioned: one above W, two inside W (left and right), one below W
Currently:
- N is one piece: rows 29-49, cols 41-49 (126 pixels)
- W is at rows 41-55, cols 41-55
- The N piece overlaps with W in rows 41-49, cols 41-49
The character needs to be "split" by the white block. At step 16, the white block was at rows 32-46, and the character was positioned such that it was split into pieces around/through the white block.
Looking at the current state, the white block is at rows 41-55, cols 41-55. The character is at rows 29-49, cols 41-49. So the character's bottom (rows 41-49) overlaps with the white block's top (rows 41-55).
I think the character needs to move further DOWN so that more of it is inside the white block, causing it to split. Let me try moving DOWN multiple times.
Actually, looking at the successful state at step 16, the white block was at rows 32-46, and the character pieces were:
- rows 29-31 (above W)
- rows 35-37 cols 41-43 (inside W, left)
- rows 35-37 cols 47-49 (inside W, right)
- rows 47-49 (below W)
So the character "passed through" the white block, leaving pieces behind. The white block is 15 rows tall (32-46), and the character pieces are positioned at the top, middle-left, middle-right, and bottom of the white block.
Currently, the white block is at rows 41-55. If I move the character DOWN so that it's positioned similarly (with pieces above, inside-left, inside-right, and below the white block), then SPACE should work.
Let me try moving DOWN to see if the character splits.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try moving DOWN multiple times to get character into the white block
predict(expect_change=True, note='DOWN to move character into white block')
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
